title: Ludwigia irwinii
taxonomy: Ludwigia irwinii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. It is a member of the Onagraceae family, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the evening primrose or willowherb family. Within the family Onagraceae, Ludwigia irwinii belongs to the tribe Ludwigieae. This tribe is further divided into the genera Ludwigia and Neoludwigia. Ludwigia irwinii is also known to be a part of the genus Ludwigia.
uses: Ludwigia irwinii is a plant species native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. However, its medicinal properties and uses are not well-documented. Further research is required to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential. Ludwigia irwinii has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in some studies, but more studies are needed to confirm these findings. While it may have some medicinal uses, the plant should be used with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
